mr-d-rollo-v-marstons-trading-ltd-2024-0.lesson: ChatGPT conversation logs are not expert evidence; tribunals will exclude them outright.

munchang-choi-v-lloyds-register-canada-l.lesson: Over 30 fabricated citations in a single reply still drew only admonishment for pro-se filers; attorneys will not be so lucky.

lakaev-v-mcconkey-2024-07-12.lesson: Appeals die on fabricated citations even when formal sanctions are withheld from unrepresented parties.

x-bv-in-z-v-tax-inspector-2024-06-26.lesson: If you cannot produce the exact prompt and verification trail, expect Dutch courts to discard the AI-derived argument entirely.

x-bv-in-z-v-tax-inspector-2024-03-05.lesson: Log every ChatGPT prompt and output; unverifiable AI statements carry zero evidentiary weight.

finch-v-the-heat-group-2024-02-27.lesson: Pro-se filers presenting 24 fabricated authorities still collapse the application; treat any uncited list as suspect on intake.

zhang-v-chen-2024-02-20.lesson: BC Supreme Court equated fake AI citations with making a false statement to the court - costs attach and careers follow.

harber-v-hmrc-2023-12-04.lesson: UK tribunals excused the pro-se taxpayer but telegraphed that solicitors filing the same fake cases will be punished.

parker-v-forsyth-nno-and-others-2023-06-.lesson: Punitive attorney-and-client costs landed for ChatGPT citations even after candid concession - verify before you file, not after.

nash-v-director-of-public-prosecutions-2.lesson: Appellate courts now flag ChatGPT-style fake authorities sua sponte; screen pro-se cited cases before oral argument.

nº-0600814-8520226000000-2023-04-14.lesson: Brazilian electoral court fined an attorney for bad-faith ChatGPT submissions - novelty is no defense when the filing is manifestly unfounded.
